text.title: AI Incident Triage Matrix Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Two raters score same incident 2 vs 4. Mitigation routing diverges. Postmortem stalls.
antipattern.fix: Write anchors at 1/3/5 minimum before scoring opens. Reject score submissions against unanchored dimensions.

antipattern.symptom: Weights tuned after scoring to produce the desired lane. Rubric becomes opinion-laundering.
antipattern.fix: Pre-register weights in a signed PR before scoring. Any weight change requires explicit reviewer + retro entry.

antipattern.symptom: 30% of incidents tagged 'other'. No team owns mitigation. Same incidents recur.
antipattern.root-cause: Output schema permits 'other' as lane value.
antipattern.fix: Drop 'other' from enum. Force operator to gather more evidence until classification is supportable.

antipattern.symptom: $50k decision scored by one rater. Mitigation later overturned by team retro.
antipattern.root-cause: Two-rater rule waived for time pressure.
antipattern.fix: Hard gate: rubric script rejects submission with rater_count<2 when stake_class='strategic' OR cost_impact>=10000.
